meta.purpose: Expansion of investigation into NEW angles not yet covered in existing research files
meta.excludes: SIDE intelligence funds, Belocopitt network, health sector cartel, Glencore mining (already in side-belocopitt-health-findings.json)
meta.methodology: WebSearch across Argentine media, judicial sources, FARA databases, transparency organizations

findings.summary_en: Active federal investigation into President Milei's promotion of $LIBRA cryptocurrency on Feb 14, 2025, which collapsed within hours. Phone forensics reveal a sustained financial relationship between Milei and intermediary Mauricio Novelli dating to 2021, and a draft contract for US$5M in exchange for presidential endorsement.
findings.summary_es: Investigación federal activa por la promoción de Milei de la criptomoneda $LIBRA el 14/02/2025, que colapsó en horas. Peritajes telefónicos revelan relación financiera sostenida entre Milei y el intermediario Mauricio Novelli desde 2021, y un borrador de contrato por US$5M a cambio del respaldo presidencial.

key_facts: Promoted $LIBRA on X on Feb 14, 2025; token collapsed within 3 hours
Draft confidential agreement found on Novelli's phone dated Jan 29, 2025
US$5M payment structure: US$1.5M upfront, US$1.5M on social media announcement, US$2M on signing in-person contract
Phone forensics reveal calls and payments from Novelli to Milei dating back to 2021 when Milei was a deputy
Prosecutor Eduardo Taiano delayed incorporating phone evidence for 4 months (seized March, analyzed October, incorporated post-elections)
Government denied existence of contract but did not deny contact
entries.potential_charges: Abuse of authority, fraud, influence trafficking, bribery
entries.court: Federal Court No. 8, Judge Marcelo Martínez de Giorgi, Prosecutor Eduardo Taiano

entries.role: Trader, lobbista, intermediario entre Milei y Davis
key_facts: Charged Hayden Davis to connect him with President Milei
Phone seized in March 2025, contained draft contract and extensive communications
Financial relationship with Milei documented since 2021 (payments for courses and market activities)
Acted within commercial scheme that resulted in alleged million-dollar crypto scams
Ideologist of the $LIBRA token
Signed 'irrevocable offer' with Novelli and CNV advisor Sergio Morales on Nov 21, 2024
Morales (CNV advisor) was to receive US$1.55M + 35% of Kelsier Group billing from clients he brought

entries.role: Asesor de la Comisión Nacional de Valores (CNV)
key_facts: Signed agreement with Davis and Novelli on Nov 21, 2024
Was to receive US$1.55M + 35% of Kelsier Group billing
Securities regulator advisor receiving payments from crypto venture he should oversee

findings.summary_en: Demian Reidel resigned as president of Nucleoeléctrica Argentina (NASA) in Feb 2026 after multiple overpricing scandals. A software contract ballooned from US$600K to US$7M (1,066% overpricing). Maintenance contracts showed 130%+ overpricing. Connected to government's nuclear/energy ambitions.
findings.summary_es: Demian Reidel renunció como presidente de Nucleoeléctrica Argentina en feb 2026 tras múltiples escándalos de sobreprecios. Un contrato de software pasó de US$600K a US$7M (1.066% de sobreprecio). Contratos de mantenimiento con más del 130% de sobreprecio.

entries.role: Ex-presidente de Nucleoeléctrica Argentina S.A. (designado abril 2025, renunció feb 2026)
key_facts: Software contract: US$600K → US$7M (1,066.7% overpricing)
Nuclear plant maintenance contracts with 130%+ overpricing
Two managers suspended for their role in overpriced tenders
Reidel voted AGAINST suspending the managers, raising questions about his role
Replaced by Juan Martín Campos
Also investigated for cancellation of personal debts with large sums

findings.section: Fundación Faro — Dark Money Electoral Advertising
findings.summary_en: Fundación Faro, led by Agustín Laje (close Milei ally), spent ARS$1.079B (US$821K) on electoral advertising on Meta platforms in 2025-2026 without declaring fund origins. Never filed financial statements with IGJ. Second-largest political advertiser on Facebook/Instagram after the Cabinet Office. Built on shell of renamed predecessor foundation.
findings.summary_es: Fundación Faro, dirigida por Agustín Laje (aliado cercano de Milei), gastó $1.079M (US$821K) en publicidad electoral en plataformas Meta en 2025-2026 sin declarar origen de fondos. Nunca presentó balances ante la IGJ. Segunda mayor anunciante político en Facebook/Instagram después de Jefatura de Gabinete.

entries.role: Director Ejecutivo de Fundación Faro Argentina
entries.entity: Fundación Faro Argentina / Ratio Oficial (media arm)
key_facts: ARS$1.079B (US$821K) spent on 15,000+ posts classified as political/electoral advertising by Meta
Second-largest political advertiser on Facebook/Instagram in Argentina
Never filed financial statements with Inspección General de Justicia (IGJ)
Built on renamed predecessor foundation — all previous board members resigned simultaneously
Refused to disclose funding sources, whether foreign donations exist, or total income for 2024-2025
Described as 'caja negra de la batalla cultural' (black box of the culture war)

findings.section: YPF 'Stealth Advertising' — Santiago Caputo's Media Control Machine
findings.summary_en: While declaring 'zero official advertising,' the Milei government channeled ~US$100M through YPF, Aerolíneas Argentinas, and Banco Nación, controlled by Santiago Caputo. YPF ad spending was the highest since its 2012 nationalization. Caputo placed his consulting firm partner Guillermo Garat as YPF VP to control distribution. YPF refuses to reveal which media receive funds, defying judicial orders.
findings.summary_es: Mientras declaraba 'pauta cero,' el gobierno Milei canalizó ~US$100M a través de YPF, Aerolíneas Argentinas y Banco Nación, controlados por Santiago Caputo. El gasto publicitario de YPF fue el más alto desde su nacionalización en 2012. Caputo colocó a su socio Guillermo Garat como VP de YPF para controlar la distribución.

entries.role: Asesor presidencial / controlador de facto de pauta estatal
key_facts: Controls advertising flow through three state companies: YPF, Aerolíneas Argentinas, Banco Nación
YPF 2024 ad spend: ARS$97.1B (~US$100M), up from ARS$31.7B in 2023 (40% real increase)
Highest YPF advertising spend since 2012 nationalization
Placed consulting firm partner Guillermo Garat as YPF VP to control media distribution
Advertising channeled through intermediaries: Alejandra Rafuls consulting firm and the Noguer brothers
YPF refuses to disclose recipient media, defying Public Information Access Agency rulings
Constitutes de facto discretionary official advertising while claiming 'zero advertising' policy
entries.connection_to_existing: Santiago Caputo already in investigation for SIDE fund control. This reveals parallel media control through state company advertising budgets.

entries.role: Vicepresidente de YPF / Socio de consultora de Caputo
key_facts: Business partner of Santiago Caputo's consulting firm
Placed as YPF VP to execute Caputo's advertising distribution decisions
Decides which media and projects receive YPF sponsorship money

findings.section: AFA Gate — Football Money Laundering Network
findings.summary_en: Massive investigation into AFA president Claudio 'Chiqui' Tapia and financier Ariel Vallejo through Sur Finanzas. 60+ raids conducted. ARS$19.5B in retained employee/player contributions not remitted to tax authorities. US$19M routed to the US through contracts bypassing Argentina. Tapia and treasurer Toviggino prohibited from leaving the country.
findings.summary_es: Investigación masiva contra el presidente de la AFA Claudio 'Chiqui' Tapia y el financista Ariel Vallejo a través de Sur Finanzas. 60+ allanamientos realizados. $19.500M en aportes retenidos no remitidos al fisco. US$19M enviados a EE.UU. mediante contratos eludiendo Argentina.

entries.role: Presidente de la AFA (Asociación del Fútbol Argentino)
key_facts: Cited for testimony on charges of retaining pension contributions and tax evasion
Prohibited from leaving Argentina
AFA acted as 'retention agent' for player/employee money and failed to remit ARS$19.5B to tax authorities
US$19M that should have been deposited in Argentina were sent to the US through contracts
Government filed complaint in the US for hidden money circuit and millionaire diversions
Connected to Sur Finanzas through financier Ariel Vallejo

entries.role: Financista vinculado a Tapia / Sur Finanzas
key_facts: Principal suspect in money laundering investigation
Strong involvement in football business
30 raids on Sur Finanzas offices in Feb 2026
4 new defendants charged, released but remain under investigation
Cited for testimony alongside Tapia
Prohibited from leaving Argentina
Linked to match-fixing scandal involving referee Luis Lobo Medina and Juan Pablo Beacon

findings.section: Adorni Private Flight Scandal — Possible Bribery
findings.summary_en: Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni flew with his family on a private jet (Honda Jet LVHWA83, operated by Alphacentauri S.A.) to Punta del Este during Carnival 2026, paid for by journalist-businessman Marcelo Grandio's company Imhouse SA. Under investigation by Judge Ariel Lijo and Prosecutor Gerardo Pollicita for possible passive bribery. Adorni's wife also flew on the presidential plane to New York.
findings.summary_es: El Jefe de Gabinete Manuel Adorni voló con su familia en jet privado a Punta del Este durante Carnaval 2026, pagado por la empresa Imhouse SA del periodista-empresario Marcelo Grandio. Investigado por el juez Ariel Lijo y el fiscal Gerardo Pollicita por posible cohecho pasivo.

key_facts: Private flight on Honda Jet LVHWA83 from San Fernando airport, Feb 12, 2026
Flight operated by Alphacentauri S.A., cost $4,830
Paid by Imhouse SA (Marcelo Grandio's production company)
Grandio gave contradictory statements about payment
Justice ordered review of cameras, flight invoices, and tax secrecy lifted for production company and pilot
Adorni's wife Bettina Angeletti also traveled on presidential plane to NY despite Adorni previously announcing end of family member flights on official aircraft
Government launched internal investigation to find who leaked the information
entries.potential_charges: Passive bribery, acceptance of gifts (prohibited for officials from persons/companies with state ties)
entries.court: Judge Ariel Lijo, Prosecutor Gerardo Pollicita

key_facts: Paid for the private flight through his company Imhouse SA
Gave contradictory statements about who ultimately paid
Tax secrecy lifted by judicial order

findings.summary_en: SIGEN audit (Sept 2025) found the Rosario-Sunchales highway project on Ruta Nacional 34 went from ARS$447M budget to ARS$40.6B actual cost — a 9,000% increase (90x original amount). Project took 10+ years. Contract object was fundamentally altered mid-execution. No periodic audits conducted during entire construction period.
findings.summary_es: Auditoría de SIGEN (sept 2025) encontró que la autopista Rosario-Sunchales en Ruta Nacional 34 pasó de un presupuesto de $447M a un costo real de $40.600M — un aumento de 9.000% (90 veces el monto original). Proyecto demoró 10+ años.

key_facts: Original budget (2010): ARS$447,024,359
Construction began 2014, only enabled 2025 (10+ years)
Project changed mid-execution from highway to expressway with 130km/h design speed
No periodic technical or financial audits conducted by Vialidad Nacional
External inspections were 'partial and untimely'
SIGEN concluded: 'substantial contractual alterations' and 'distorted original economic equation'

findings.section: FGS Pension Fund — Proposed Asset Liquidation
findings.summary_en: The FGS (Sustainability Guarantee Fund) holds ARS$84.2T (US$62.2B) in retiree pension assets. Milei's 'Bases' law attempted to transfer FGS assets to Treasury and eliminate restrictions on stock operations. Business leaders lobbied for selling FGS stock portfolio (valued at US$3-13B depending on methodology) for infrastructure. 79.8% invested in government debt. Potential conflict: pension fund used to finance government while retirees receive diminished benefits.
findings.summary_es: El FGS tiene $84,2 billones (US$62.200M) en activos jubilatorios. La ley 'Bases' de Milei intentó transferir activos del FGS al Tesoro y eliminar restricciones sobre operaciones con acciones. Empresarios presionaron para vender cartera de acciones del FGS (valuada entre US$3.000-13.000M según metodología) para infraestructura.

key_facts: Total assets: ARS$84.2T (US$62.2B) as of August 2025
79.8% invested in national/provincial government debt
80% of assets denominated in pesos, only 20% in dollars
Stock portfolio valuation discrepancy: US$8.3B (previous government) vs US$3B (current government) — needs independent audit
Bases law attempted to flexibilize conditions for using FGS investments
Business leaders repeatedly discussed selling FGS shares for infrastructure funding with Milei officials
IMF agreement requires pension system reform by December 2026

findings.section: RIGI Mining Regime — Fiscal Sovereignty Concerns
findings.summary_en: The RIGI (Large Investment Incentive Regime) grants mining companies 30-year fiscal stability, reduces income tax from 35% to 25%, exempts capital goods imports from tariffs. US$24.8B in projects approved, US$33.9B total presented. Critics note: no environmental protections, risks of deindustrialization, prioritizes corporate water access over communities. Lithium royalties only 3% on boca de mina value, export retention 4.5%. Less than 15% of lithium revenue stays in Argentina.
findings.summary_es: El RIGI otorga a mineras estabilidad fiscal por 30 años, reduce impuesto a las ganancias de 35% a 25%, exime importaciones de bienes de capital. US$24.800M en proyectos aprobados. Críticas: sin protección ambiental, riesgos de desindustrialización. Regalías del litio solo 3%, retenciones 4,5%. Menos del 15% de ingresos del litio quedan en Argentina.

entries.role: Régimen de Incentivo para Grandes Inversiones (RIGI)
key_facts: 30-year fiscal and exchange rate stability guarantee
Income tax reduced from 35% to 25%
Capital goods import tariff exemption
9 projects approved totaling US$24.8B; 10 more pending
Mining exports hit record US$6B in 2025
Lithium royalties: 3% of boca de mina value only
Lithium export retention: 4.5% vs soy 30-35%
Less than 15% of lithium revenue (US$1.1B in 2023) stays in Argentina
Transfer pricing concerns: lithium transactions are private, prices not easily verifiable
No environmental protections in RIGI articles
Prioritizes corporate water access over community needs
Government debating modification of Glaciers Law to unlock US$30B in mining investments
entries.connection_to_existing: Glencore already in investigation for offshore structures and El Pachón/MARA RIGI applications (US$14B combined). RIGI framework enables the tax optimization already documented.

findings.summary_en: NEW: In March 2026, the government quietly rewrote the National Intelligence Plan (PIN) to remove the most controversial passages enabling domestic espionage against journalists and opponents. The original 170-page secret document targeted anyone who 'erodes confidence' in officials. Journalist Hugo Alconada Mon reported hacking attempts after publishing PIN details. New version sent to Congress — but core surveillance infrastructure (DNU 941/2025) remains intact.
findings.summary_es: NUEVO: En marzo 2026, el gobierno reescribió silenciosamente el Plan de Inteligencia Nacional para eliminar pasajes más controversiales. El documento secreto original de 170 páginas apuntaba a quien 'erosione la confianza' en funcionarios. Periodista Hugo Alconada Mon reportó intentos de hackeo tras publicar detalles.

key_facts: Published details of the secret 170-page National Intelligence Plan
Reported hacking attempts on social media accounts after publication
Government subsequently rewrote the PIN removing most controversial passages
New version sent to Congress in March 2026
Core surveillance infrastructure from DNU 941/2025 remains intact despite revisions
entries.connection_to_existing: Updates existing SIDE investigation. Shows reactive pattern — government modifies only after exposure, underlying power structure unchanged.

findings.section: IMF Agreement — US$57.1B Debt and Governance Conditions
findings.summary_en: Argentina signed a new 48-month Extended Fund Facility with the IMF in April 2025 for US$20B, with an unprecedented US$12B immediate disbursement (60%). Total debt to IMF: US$57.1B as of Dec 2025. Massive upcoming obligations: ~US$18B in 2026, averaging US$28B/year for 2026-2028. Conditions include pension reform by Dec 2026 and fiscal surplus targets rising to 2.5% GDP. The scale creates structural dependency that constrains policy choices.
findings.summary_es: Argentina firmó nuevo acuerdo EFF de 48 meses con el FMI en abril 2025 por US$20.000M, con desembolso inmediato sin precedentes de US$12.000M (60%). Deuda total con FMI: US$57.100M a dic 2025. Obligaciones: ~US$18.000M en 2026, promedio US$28.000M/año 2026-2028.

key_facts: New EFF: US$20B over 48 months (April 2025)
Immediate disbursement: US$12B (60%) — unprecedented in EFF history
Total IMF debt: US$57.1B (Dec 2025)
2026 repayment obligations: ~US$18B
2026-2028 average annual repayments: US$28B
Conditions: pension reform by Dec 2026, fiscal surplus 1.3% GDP (2025) → 2.5% GDP
Social coverage requirement: AUH and Tarjeta Alimentar at 95% of basic food basket
Creates structural dependency constraining domestic policy

findings.summary_en: Argentina's Corruption Perception Index score dropped to 36/100 in 2025 (from 37 in 2024), ranking 104th globally. Transparency International noted more corruption cases in 2025 than in the previous five years combined. Nearly 1,800 open corruption cases in Argentina's courts.
findings.summary_es: El Índice de Percepción de Corrupción de Argentina bajó a 36/100 en 2025 (desde 37 en 2024), puesto 104 global. Transparencia Internacional señaló más casos de corrupción en 2025 que en los cinco años anteriores combinados. Casi 1.800 causas abiertas por corrupción.

key_facts: Global rank: 104th
More corruption cases in 2025 than previous 5 years
1,800 open corruption cases in Argentine courts (ACIJ data)
Axel Kicillof (Buenos Aires governor) has 17 corruption complaints

cross_references.description_en: CNV advisor Sergio Morales was contracted by $LIBRA creator Hayden Davis for US$1.55M + 35% of revenue. This shows crypto promoters buying access to the very securities regulator tasked with protecting investors — regulatory capture at its most direct.
cross_references.description_es: El asesor de la CNV Sergio Morales fue contratado por el creador de $LIBRA Hayden Davis por US$1.55M + 35% de ingresos. Los promotores de cripto compraron acceso directo al regulador de valores encargado de proteger inversores.

cross_references.connection: Santiago Caputo: SIDE + YPF + media = triple control nexus
cross_references.description_en: Santiago Caputo controls three levers simultaneously: (1) SIDE intelligence funds (ARS$97B+), (2) YPF advertising distribution (~US$100M), (3) de facto media ecosystem through discretionary funding. Combined with DNU 941 surveillance powers, this creates unprecedented control over information flow — spy on critics, fund allies, silence opponents.
cross_references.description_es: Santiago Caputo controla tres palancas simultáneamente: (1) fondos SIDE ($97.000M+), (2) distribución publicitaria YPF (~US$100M), (3) ecosistema mediático de facto mediante financiamiento discrecional. Combinado con poderes de vigilancia del DNU 941, control sin precedentes del flujo de información.

cross_references.connection: Fundación Faro ↔ YPF pauta ↔ electoral financing
cross_references.description_en: While Milei claims 'zero official advertising,' his ecosystem operates through: (1) Fundación Faro spending ARS$1.079B on undisclosed-origin electoral ads, (2) YPF channeling ARS$97.1B through Caputo-controlled intermediaries. Together this creates a parallel electoral financing system that circumvents campaign finance law.
cross_references.description_es: Mientras Milei proclama 'pauta cero,' su ecosistema opera mediante: (1) Fundación Faro gastando $1.079M en publicidad electoral de origen no declarado, (2) YPF canalizando $97.100M a través de intermediarios de Caputo. Juntos crean un sistema paralelo de financiamiento electoral.

cross_references.connection: RIGI + Glencore offshore = institutional enablement of tax avoidance
cross_references.description_en: The RIGI regime (25% income tax, 30-year stability, import exemptions) institutionalizes the tax optimization that Glencore's offshore structures (Bermuda/Cayman) already enabled informally. Combined with 3% royalties on artificially low boca de mina values and transfer pricing through private transactions, the result is that less than 15% of Argentina's lithium/mining revenue stays in the country.
cross_references.description_es: El régimen RIGI (25% ganancias, estabilidad 30 años, exención importaciones) institucionaliza la optimización fiscal que las estructuras offshore de Glencore ya habilitaban informalmente. Con regalías de 3% sobre valores artificialmente bajos y precios de transferencia, menos del 15% de ingresos mineros quedan en Argentina.

cross_references.description_en: The 1,066% software overpricing at Nucleoeléctrica mirrors the pattern already documented in defense procurement (VEMERKIPER SRL shell company with 13 contracts). State enterprises with strategic/security mandates serve as vehicles for overpricing schemes precisely because their 'strategic' status reduces external scrutiny.
cross_references.description_es: El sobreprecio de 1.066% en software de Nucleoeléctrica replica el patrón documentado en contrataciones de defensa (VEMERKIPER SRL, empresa fantasma con 13 contratos). Empresas estatales con mandatos estratégicos/de seguridad sirven como vehículos de sobreprecio porque su carácter 'estratégico' reduce el escrutinio externo.

cross_references.description_en: The IMF requires pension reform by Dec 2026 while the FGS holds US$62.2B in assets that the government has attempted to transfer to Treasury control. The combination of IMF structural adjustment demands and Milei's desire to liquidate FGS shareholdings for infrastructure creates a direct threat to pension fund integrity — retirees' assets could be sold to meet fiscal targets.
cross_references.description_es: El FMI exige reforma previsional para dic 2026 mientras el FGS tiene US$62.200M en activos que el gobierno intentó transferir al Tesoro. La combinación de ajuste estructural del FMI y el deseo de Milei de liquidar participaciones del FGS para infraestructura amenaza la integridad del fondo jubilatorio.

investigation_priority_ranking.reason: Active federal investigation with direct presidential involvement, documented financial trail, potential US$5M bribery, CNV regulatory capture. Most explosive potential.

investigation_priority_ranking.topic: Santiago Caputo triple nexus (SIDE + YPF + media)
investigation_priority_ranking.reason: Single individual controlling intelligence, advertising, and media — unprecedented concentration. ARS$194B+ in combined funds under one non-official's control.
US$821K in electoral advertising with zero financial transparency. Potential illegal campaign financing. IGJ non-compliance. Easy to investigate via Meta Ad Library.
US$19M diverted to US, ARS$19.5B in retained contributions. Active raids. Cross-border money laundering angle provides international jurisdiction.
US$24.8B in approved projects with 30-year locked-in tax breaks. Less than 15% of mining revenue stays in Argentina. Connects to existing Glencore offshore investigation.
1,066% software overpricing is documented and provoked resignation. Pattern connects to defense procurement anomalies already in investigation.
US$62.2B in retiree assets at risk. IMF conditionality creates pressure to restructure. US$5.3B valuation discrepancy needs audit.
90x cost overrun documented by SIGEN. Pattern of infrastructure corruption spanning multiple administrations.
